constraints: Given an array BBB of length MMM, consider the following operation on it:
Choose LLL and RRR such that:
1≤L≤R≤M1 \le L \le R \le M1≤L≤R≤M
Bi=BLB_i = B_LBi​=BL​ for all L≤i≤RL \le i \le RL≤i≤R
L=1L = 1L=1 or BL≠BL−1B_L \neq B_{L-1}BL​=BL−1​
R=MR = MR=M or BR≠BR+1B_R \neq B_{R+1}BR​=BR+1​
Then, do one of the following 222 actions:
for every L≤j≤RL \le j \le RL≤j≤R, modify BjB_jBj​ to Bj+1B_j + 1Bj​+1; **or**
for every L≤j≤RL \le j \le RL≤j≤R, modify BjB_jBj​ to Bj−1B_j - 1Bj​−1.

Less formally, an operation is choosing a range of equal values in the array AAA that's maximal (meaning the range can't be further extended left or right without including a different value), and either adding 111 to each element, or subtracting 111 from each element.
Note that in a single operation, you are not allowed to add 111 to some values and subtract 111 from others: every value in the chosen range must be modified in the same way.
For an integer KKK, we define f(B,K)f(B, K)f(B,K) to be the minimum number of operations required to ensure that the sum of adjacent differences of BBB doesn't exceed KKK, that is,
∑i=2M∣Bi−Bi−1∣≤K\sum_{i=2}^M |B_i - B_{i-1}| \leq Ki=2∑M​∣Bi​−Bi−1​∣≤KYou are given an array AAA of length NNN. Answer QQQ queries on it:
Given L,R,L, R, L,R, and KKK, find f(A[L…,R],K)f(A[L\ldots, R], K)f(A[L…,R],K).
A[L…R]A[L\ldots R]A[L…R] refers to the subarray [AL,AL+1,…,AR][A_L, A_{L+1}, \ldots, A_R][AL​,AL+1​,…,AR​] of AAA.
Input Format
The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
The first line contains NNN and QQQ - the size of the array and the number of queries, respectively.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​
The next QQQ lines contain 333 integers each - LLL, RRR and KKK, the parameters of each query.
Output Format
For each query, output on a new line f(A[L…R],K)f(A[L\ldots R], K)f(A[L…R],K)
1≤L<R≤N1 \le L \lt R \le N1≤L<R≤N

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We have A=[1,6,4]A = [1, 6, 4]A=[1,6,4].

Query 111: We perform the following operations

Choose L=2,R=2L = 2, R = 2L=2,R=2 and decrement by 111 to get A=[1,5,4]A = [1, 5, 4]A=[1,5,4]
Choose L=1,R=1L = 1, R = 1L=1,R=1 and increment by 111 to get A=[2,5,4]A = [2, 5, 4]A=[2,5,4]
Choose L=1,R=1L = 1, R = 1L=1,R=1 and increment by 111 to get A=[3,5,4]A = [3, 5, 4]A=[3,5,4]
Choose L=2,R=2L = 2, R = 2L=2,R=2 and decrement by 111 to get A=[3,4,4]A = [3, 4, 4]A=[3,4,4]
At the end of these 4 operations, you can note that ∑i=23∣Ai−Ai−1∣=1≤K(=1)\sum_{i=2}^3 |A_i - A_{i - 1}| = 1 \le K (=1)∑i=23​∣Ai​−Ai−1​∣=1≤K(=1) holds.



Test case 333: We have A=[1,1,1,3]A = [1, 1, 1, 3]A=[1,1,1,3].

Query 111:

Choose L=1,R=3L = 1, R = 3L=1,R=3 and increment by 111 (note that this is a maximal segment of equal elements). Then A=[2,2,2,3]A = [2, 2, 2, 3]A=[2,2,2,3].
The sum of adjacent differences is 111.
